question: An agent must rename a function used in 40 files across a monorepo. What is the correct strategy versus manually editing each file one by one?
A large refactor touches 200 files. Should this be one giant commit, or broken into smaller commits? Explain the tradeoffs for review and rollback.
Midway through a large refactor, the agent realizes the original plan has a flaw that will require redoing 30% of the work already done. What should it do?
A large-scale refactor changes a shared interface. How should the agent verify it hasn't broken any of the 40 files that depend on that interface, beyond just 'it compiles'?
Should a large refactor be done on a long-lived feature branch, or in small increments merged directly to master behind a flag? Explain when each is appropriate.
